methodsAttempted.query: site:unige.ch University of Geneva generative AI artificial intelligence policy students teaching assessment data privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found central UNIGE general-policy pages, Faculty of Science AI directive pages, a continuing-education course page, a position-paper PDF, and older or advisory materials.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unige.ch/en/university/politique-generale/ UNIGE generative artificial intelligence statement AI position paper
methodsAttempted.notes: Confirmed the official central Statement on artificial intelligence page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unige.ch intelligence artificielle generative UNIGE etudiants enseignants donnees sensibles examens charte
methodsAttempted.notes: French-language discovery found CIEL and campus/news materials, but central English UNIGE pages and Faculty of Science English pages were more directly usable for staged claims.
Followed official page links to the Digital University practical guide risks page, Faculty of Science directive, recommendations, declaration templates, and UNIGE Library referencing guide.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unige.ch UNIGE position paper artificial intelligence PDF
methodsAttempted.notes: Official PDFs were discovered but not needed after current HTML policy pages were available; duplicate or broad magazine/report PDFs were excluded.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found central UNIGE policy/guidance pages on artificial intelligence and generative AI, a Digital University practical guide risks page, and Faculty of Science directive material. Course marketing, news/archive guide notices, magazine PDFs, duplicate PDFs, and low-specificity template pages were documented but not used as approved claim evidence.

stopConditions: Do not treat Faculty of Science material as university-wide unless the claim is explicitly scoped to that faculty.
Do not use course marketing, news notices, duplicate PDFs, or broad magazine articles as binding policy evidence.
Do not stage raw HTML, PDF, screenshots, or non-JSON artifacts.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow institution-level AI treatment claim directly supported by the central UNIGE statement.
Approved as an academic-integrity responsibility claim because the central UNIGE statement assigns responsibility and final validation to users.
Approved as a privacy/legal caution claim directly supported by UNIGE's generative AI principles.
Approved as a teaching-governance claim because the central statement assigns AI integration and conditions to faculties and interfaculty centers.
Approved as a teaching and assessment claim because the source says academic work and learning assessment must include clear instructions and supervised use.
Approved as an academic-integrity transparency claim directly supported by the central UNIGE generative AI page.
Approved as a privacy-risk guidance claim based on the official Digital University practical guide risks page.
Approved only as a Faculty of Science-scoped academic-integrity claim; the claim text preserves that scope.
Approved only as a Faculty of Science-scoped teaching claim; the claim text preserves that scope.
Approved only as a Faculty of Science-scoped privacy claim; the evidence says sensitive personal data must not be disclosed to public AI tools without confidentiality.

artifacts.title: University of Geneva AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: University of Geneva official sources provide central claims on responsible AI use, legality and confidentiality, faculty-level governance of teaching conditions, clear instructions for generative AI use in academic work and assessment, citation transparency, and Digital University privacy-risk guidance. Faculty of Science sources provide narrower faculty-scoped claims on declaration requirements, teacher authorization or prohibition of AI use, and sensitive-data limits for public AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Faculty of Science source claims are scoped to that faculty and are not asserted as university-wide rules.
Course marketing, news/archive notices, magazine PDFs, duplicate PDFs, and low-specificity template pages were not used as approved claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
